profiles.settings_route_body: Search mapped controls and apply policy changes without opening the JSON editor.
profiles.settings_mode_review_body: Fix items that need attention first.
profiles.settings_list_body: Scan schema policies and managed preferences together before opening a specific control below.
profiles.settings_list_summary_filtered: {visible} shown of {total} entries • {configured} configured • {policies} policies • {preferences} managed preferences
profiles.settings_list_empty: This schema does not expose any settings here yet.
profiles.settings_a11y_domain_card: {title}: {configured} configured, {attention} need attention, {available} available, {mapped} mapped, {raw} raw fallback, {deprecated} deprecated.
profiles.settings_review_body: Review imported, deprecated, raw fallback, and validation-sensitive settings before handoff.
profiles.settings_review_success_body: This profile has no configured technical review items. Continue with configured settings or browse the full catalog.
profiles.settings_review_cis_body: CIS merge decisions that need an admin choice or exception note should be reviewed before handoff.
profiles.settings_review_unknown_body: Top-level keys outside the active schema are preserved and should be verified.
profiles.settings_review_deprecated_body: Configured older policy keys remain saved but deserve a compatibility check.
profiles.settings_review_raw_body: Configured schema items without a full visual editor stay editable through raw JSON.
profiles.settings_review_invalid_body: Entries with schema validation messages need a closer look before handoff.
profiles.settings_detail_empty: Select a setting to inspect and edit it.
profiles.settings_detail_raw_json_error: Raw values must be valid JSON.
profiles.settings_detail_validation_clea: No validation issues for this setting.
profiles.settings_advanced_schema_body: Open full schema-shell coverage for this domain when you need raw or advanced controls.
profiles.settings_category_browser_acces: Core browser behavior, downloads, updates, proxy, authentication, and enterprise access.
profiles.settings_category_home_startup_: Homepage, new-tab behavior, startup pages, and Firefox Home surfaces.
profiles.settings_category_search_naviga: Default engines, managed search, suggestions, and address-bar behavior.
profiles.settings_category_privacy_secur: Telemetry, permissions, cookies, site data, hardening, and cleanup controls.
profiles.settings_category_users_addons_: Mozilla accounts, language, add-ons, bookmarks, handlers, and website controls.
profiles.settings_category_ai_smart_feat: AI controls, generative features, visual search, and related smart surfaces.
profiles.settings_category_raw_unmapped_: Schema-backed items that stay outside the guided surfaces or need raw review.
profiles.settings_search_placeholder: Search by control, policy key, preference, or settings area
profiles.settings_search_hint: Search the All settings catalog by policy, preference, or mapped Firefox area.
profiles.settings_search_empty: No matching settings found in All settings.
profiles.settings_map_body: Use these clusters to jump straight to the controls that cover this area.
profiles.settings_preferences_empty: No managed preferences have been added in this section yet.
profiles.settings_context_body: Use All settings for the full visual catalog, lower-level browser controls, detailed coverage, and deeper inspection when Guided editor is no longer enough.
profiles.settings_context_from_step: You opened All settings from {step}. Finish any lower-level settings here, then return to Guided editor.
profiles.settings_context_more: More lower-level areas in this step: {count}.
profiles.settings_context_empty_title: Starting here without a step handoff?
profiles.settings_context_empty_body: Begin with profile details if this is a new draft, open the full policies.json when you already know the lower-level keys you need, or run validation first when you are inspecting an existing profile.
profiles.settings_utility_body: When you work directly in All settings, the cleanest order is profile details first, full policies.json second, and validation plus policies.json download last.
profiles.settings_utility_details_body: Name, schema, and file format belong here before you move into lower-level editing.
profiles.settings_utility_editor_body: Use the full Firefox policies.json when you already know the lower-level keys you need to manage.
profiles.settings_utility_validate_body: Validate the current file, then download policies.json only when the saved version is the one you want to hand off.
